commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bf75e5e7fafcf7c31c6034d1789fd1966ade37d7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts`, a Vitest that stubs `globalThis.fetch` and exercises the real OpenAI Chat Completions SDK request path.

PR surface: Tests +84. Total +84 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection is enough: the pinned OpenAI SDK uses ambient global fetch when no custom fetch is supplied, so this test would still pass after removing the guarded fetch option.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code repair is narrow, but missing real behavior proof keeps this external PR on the contributor/human review path rather than an automation-only repair lane.

target.title: test(openai-completions): add fetch-proof test for buildGuardedModelFetch wiring
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

PR #97228 (merged) added `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to the OpenAI Completions SDK constructor — the 16 MiB bounded-read SSRF guard. The existing test (`openai-completions.test.ts`) verifies the constructor receives a custom `fetch` via a mocked SDK.

This PR adds a **real HTTP pipeline proof** — a test that does NOT mock the OpenAI SDK, but instead stubs `globalThis.fetch` and proves the SDK's HTTP call actually routes through `buildGuardedModelFetch`. This closes the gap between "constructor option is set" and "SDK uses the custom fetch for real requests."

## Why This Change Was Made

This is the same fetch-proof pattern used in:
- #97848 (`openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts`) — proven effective by Codex review
- #97868 (`anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts`) — same pattern

Without this test, a future refactor could accidentally swap the `buildGuardedModelFetch` for a passthrough `fetch` without the mock-based test catching it — because the mock only verifies constructor options, not the actual HTTP call.

## Evidence

### Single test: real HTTP pipeline interception

`openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts`:

1. Stubs `globalThis.fetch` with `vi.stubGlobal("fetch", vi.fn(...))` — **no mock of the `openai` SDK**
2. The real SDK receives `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as its `fetch` option
3. When the SDK issues `POST /v1/chat/completions`, it calls `this.fetch(url, init)` → `buildGuardedModelFetch` → `globalThis.fetch` (our spy)
4. Returns 500 to short-circuit; SDK retries 2-3x on 500

**Captured call:**
```
URL:       https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions
Method:    POST
Body:      { model: "gpt-5.5", stream: true, messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }] }
```

All intercepted calls (including SDK retries) target the same endpoint.

### Test results

```
✓ 1 test passed (1 file)
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts
```

### Diff scope

```
1 file changed, 68 insertions(+)
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts (new)
```

### Verification commands

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts
# 1 passed ✓

npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json \
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts
# No errors ✓

pnpm format:check -- src/llm/providers/openai-completions.fetch-proof.test.ts
# All matched files use the correct format ✓
```

### What was not tested

- The 16 MiB cap behavior is exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`; this test only proves the custom fetch is wired into the SDK.
- No loopback HTTP server test — the fetch-intercept pattern is sufficient for proving the wiring (same pattern as #97628, proven 🦞).
